mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change vs 20-day forward return is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive signals yield less than 55% positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.0%, the labor market is overheated, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy and slowing the economy, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) in periods when UNRATE is at or below 4.0%, or more than 50% of such periods produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signal that the Fed is tightening into high inflation, which compresses valuations and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns: either the Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 or the hit rate of negative predictions is below 55% over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) during a monetary tightening cycle forecast a weakening economy and falling corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when the Fed is raising rates (FEDFUNDS increases over 6 months), the predictive signal from HOUST declines yields Rank IC below 0.03 or less than 55% of triggered instances produce negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding output and capacity utilization, which signals a healthy economy and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change vs 5-day forward return is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive signals yield less than 55% positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 2.5%, it crosses into a restrictive territory that slows borrowing and investment, causing a decline in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) in periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or more than 50% of such periods produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) is rising strongly while retail sales (RSAFS) are falling, it signals a disconnect between production and consumption, indicating an impending slowdown that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns: Rank IC of the interaction term below 0.03, or less than 55% of triggered instances produce negative returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion, strong gross domestic product (GDPC1) growth above 3% annualized signals peak-cycle conditions where additional growth is unsustainable, leading to mean-reversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) in quarters following GDPC1 growth above 3%, or more than 50% of such quarters produce positive returns.
